title: Tendong Gumpa
tagline: Tendong Gumpa — heritage city of Sikkim

overview.short: Tendong Dichhen Salhun Gumpa or Tendong Gumpa as commonly known, is a Buddhist monastery situated atop 8530 ft.
overview.description: Tendong Dichhen Salhun Gumpa or Tendong Gumpa as commonly known, is a Buddhist monastery situated atop 8530 ft. altitude from mean sea level (msl). This monastery is surrounded by lush green virgin reserve forest and nearest hamlet is Damthang which is at a six kilometer walking distance. Because of its seclusion, there are no resident monks/nuns in this monastery.

topPlaces.name: Damthang, Sikkim
topPlaces.description: Damthang is a small town in the Namchi district of the Indian state of Sikkim.

topPlaces.name: Perbing
topPlaces.description: Perbing is a village near Namchi in the Namchi district of Sikkim state, India, some 90 kilometres (56 mi) from the state capital Gangtok. The village is demographically dominated by the Sherpa tribe of Sikkim.

topPlaces.name: Ranguthang
topPlaces.description: Ranguthang is a small town in the Namchi district of the Indian state of Sikkim.

topPlaces.name: Chalamthang
topPlaces.description: Chalamthang is a village in Namchi district of Sikkim and is located at 27.2119° N, 88.4711° E. It is located 42 km away from Gangtok, capital of Sikkim and 98 km away from Siliguri. It is located at half an hour drive from Singtam, its nearest town.

topPlaces.name: Tarku, India
topPlaces.description: Tarku is a small village in the Namchi district of the Indian state of Sikkim.

topPlaces.name: Namchi Monastery
topPlaces.description: Namchi Monastery is a Buddhist monastery in Sikkim, northeastern India.

topPlaces.name: Paney-Phensong
topPlaces.description: Paney-Phensong is a village in Mangan subdivision, North Sikkim district, Sikkim, India. The Ministry of Home Affairs has given it a geographical code of 260904.

topPlaces.name: Namchi
topPlaces.description: Namchi is a city and the administrative headquarter of the Namchi district in the Indian state of Sikkim. The appellation Namchi means Sky (Nam) High (Chi) in Sikkimese.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tendong Gumpa?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tendong Gumpa is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tendong Gumpa?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pakyong Airport, Gangtok (18 km) and the nearest railway station is Siliguri Junction (55 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tendong Gumpa?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tendong Gumpa at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tendong Gumpa?
faq.a: Stays in Tendong Gumpa start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tendong Gumpa?
faq.a: The top attractions are Damthang, Sikkim, Perbing, Ranguthang, Chalamthang, Tarku, India.
